notes:
Starts without black screen after placing xlive.dll in game folder.  Run perfectly at 60fps with best graphics. No sound problems. No crashes at all.
Problems occurred:
- No play time tracked in Steam
- Mouse is always captured so for example…
--- I can't use Steam-Overlay
--- it's impossible to focus other open programs unless I switch to another workspace on the same screen (this might have to do with my i3 multi-monitor setup)
- Somewhat strange mouse behaviour in all kinds of menus with cursor (main menu, Pip-Boy, V.A.T.S., etc) so for example…
--- there is a weird relation between cursor position and expected action area where I can actually hover/click
--- sometimes there is a flickering border when hovering answer texts
--- sometimes I cannot select body parts in V.A.T.S. or even right-click, I must explicitly left-click on "Return" to try again
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
Upon following most of the fixes listed (obtaining the .dll file to fix black screen, editing FALLOUT.ini to fix mouse and stop some crashes) I still had an issue of crashing with combat, which was promptly solved through adding one of the .ini fixes I forgot to add. Overall, it was playable out of the vault and into the Capital Wasteland at 60 FPS on Ultra Settings. I had a mod induced crash trying to enter Megaton, however, so modding might have some issues. I did not have to do anything for my pipboy radio to work fine, however. 
--------------------
Fixes Were As Follows: {xlive.dll added to game root, downloaded from https://www.dll-files.com/download/6b78ebb4ddba9000bdbd2973f0e6cd85/xlive.dll.html?c=V2JNNUQ3M1pxYWpVKytYM0NxYmh1UT09 } {.ini fixes [General] bUseThreadedAI=1, iNumHWThreads=2, [Controls] fForegroundMouseAccelBase=0, fForegroundMouseAccelTop=0, fForegroundMouseBase=0, and fForegroundMouseMult=0}. The FALLOUT.ini file is found in $STEAM_DIR/SteamApps/compatdata/22370/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout3/FALLOUT.INI
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Used the same tweaks everyone else needed to use to get the game running. What hasn't already been mentioned is the severe performance issues with sound effects. Frame rates seem drop proportionally to the number of sound effects there are at once. The worst is during Operation Anchorage where there are constant sounds from weather or artillery firing on top of combat sounds. The only things that seem to help are avoiding sound effect heavy sequences and disabling sound in the INI file.

title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
Works good so far after these tweaks: 1) Copied "xlive.dll" to game directory "~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Fallout 3 goty". 2) Installed Wine and edited "Direct3D.reg" using: WINEPREFIX="~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/22370/pfx" regedit, then added two keys: [HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Wine\Direct3D] "VideoPciDeviceID"=dword:00000614, "VideoPciVendorID"=dword:000010de.
3) Edited "FALLOUT.INI" in "~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/22370/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout3" to have: bUseThreadedAI=1, iNumHWThreads=2.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: The mouse was a bit over sensitive, but everything was working perfectly.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
I needed to do the xlive.dll fix (I don't really consider this a fix though since this is necessary on recent versions of Windows too). The only other fixes needed for a base working game are to add mouse acceleration and multi-core fixes to FALLOUT.INI (again, also necessary on Windows) and to set winegstreamer to disabled (apparently this fixes a game crash later in the game, I haven't played long enough to confirm this though).

If you have issues with the Pipboy Radio, I found I needed to install the unofficial patch and the use protontricks to install quartz. I did find that using the pipboy radio with quartz set to native, and also having l3codecx installed via protontricks actually caused the game to crash.

In the Anchorage DLC I was having some major performance hits (it was easily dropping to less than 1fps). I tried it before adding the community patch, so maybe that fixes it.

Overall, it is almost flawless. Most of the fixes aren't unique to WINE, that said I don't see how the game could be whitelisted without the community patch.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: The xlive.dll problem is present on Windows too so you can say that the game works as expected. The Steam Controller works as expected.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
I have Fallout 3 GOTY edition from Steam and it runs perfectly. The textures look better, and  the bloom is not anywhere near as much of a problem as it is in Winders. Also, the radio stutter problem seems to be corrected. I played for several hours without any problems. I disabled AA to ensure no texture or object blinking, yet edges were crisp.

I am running Linux Mint 19. I am using an older Vishera CPU, and a Radeon R9 390. My system reads my video card as R9 290, and this because I have not installed the latest updated drivers from AMD to correct this. Instead, I’m using the video card drivers that are installed with Mint.

The best way to prepare for installing and playing Fallout 3 GOTY is to follow the instructions found here

https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/wiki/Requirements

I have not altered my Fallout 3 .ini files (yet) to customize CPU cores, threads, AI, et cetera. I have not altered my Fallout 3 executable for Large Address Awareness, either.

I have not yet placed the Large Address Argument in the Launcher (PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1)

I do NOT have WINE installed. I am using only Steam/Proton (Proton v4.2-6).

According to the above, Fallout 3 starts, runs and exits without issue (no need to force exit with Alt+F4).

NOTE: You MUST have the xlive.dll installed in your Fallout 3 folder, which is typically found here:

/home/xxUSERNAMExx/.steam/steampapps/common/Fallout 3 goty

If xlive.dll is not installed in the Fallout 3 game folder, your system will lock when you try to launch. The .steam folder is hidden, so you should set your file utility to view hidden files to find it in your Home directory. Again, the xlive.dll is placed in the Fallout 3 GOTY folder, and not in SysWOW64, which has its own xlive.dll already installed.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Works after installing GFWL. Have random white artifacts
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
This game can't be Platnum for me, because it requires a single tweak.  It definitely does not launch "out of the box."
If you're like me, and you don't have a Windows computer, don't worry, you can still make this game work.  After installing Fallout 3 through Steam, and running it for the first time, the game will lock up.  Kill the process.  After doing so, I used Lutris to obtain my xlive.dll file.  Here's how: 

Step 1: (Obviously install Lutris if you don't have it.)  Add a new game. In the Game Options tab, your executable will be this: [your path to Steam]/SteamApps/common/Fallout 3 goty/GFWLive/XLiveRedist01.02.0241.00.msi
Your Wine prefix will be [your path to Steam]/SteamApps/compatdata/22370/pfx

Step 2: In the Runner Options tab, your Wine version needs to be something recent.  If you don't know how to install Wine versions, from the main window of Lutris, click the gear icon next to "Runners."  Scroll down to Wine, and click the little box-with-a-wrench-and-gear icon.  Just tick the boxes for the latest few versions.

Step 3: Hit the "Play" button for your game in Lutris. This does not launch Fallout. This will install XBox Live files into Steam's "C_drive" for Fallout 3.

Step 4: Close Lutris and open a file manager.  Go to:
[your path to Steam]/SteamApps/compatdata/22370/pfx/drive_c/windows/syswow64/

Step 5: Copy xlive.dll

Step 6: Go to [your path to Steam]/SteamApps/common/Fallout 3 goty/

Step 7: Paste xlive.dll

Step 8: Next time you launch Fallout 3 from Steam, it should work, BUT in the launcher, go to Options, and I advise that you change HDR to Bloom.  All your other settings are up to you.

Step 9: Have fun!
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
Doesn't work out of the box but it is not Proton's fault. The engine is old and buggy and causes just as much trouble on Windows. Here are the tweaks I used:

(1) I wasn't initially able to start a New Game. Downloading an xlive.dll and putting it in the install directory fixed that

(2) Go to <Library>/compatdata/22370/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Fallout3/ and edit the FALLOUT.INI file as follows:
bUseThreadedAI=1 
iNumHavokThreads=4 

(3) Then insert this line in the FALLOUT.INI file:
iNumThreads=2

title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

cpu: AMD A8-7100 Radeon R5, 8 Compute Cores 4C+4G
notes:
I copied the xlive.dll file to the game's main folder. I modified the fallout.ini file with the following parameters:

For the random crashes and freezes
[General]
bUseThreadedAI=1
iNumHWThreads=2

For the mouse speed
[Controls]
fForegroundMouseAccelBase=0
fForegroundMouseAccelTop=0
fForegroundMouseBase=0
fForegroundMouseMult=0

For the lag in combat and using V.A.T.S.
[Audio]
bMultiThreadAudio=1
iAudioCacheSize=8192

The parameters for the audio have solved the problem of the drastic slowdown during the fights, making them finally fluid, but the sound tends to distort and delay, furthermore at the exit the game crashes and it must be forced to close, so i had to play in a window to avoid the restart of Cinnamon.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: Used " PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% " as launch option and xlive.dll installed in game root folder. There is a little bit of lag in some places without UUFO3 Patch. Additional tweaks with Protontricks will alleviate practically all of the problems. I've modded Fallout 3 on Linux with FOSE and all kinds of mods (except ENB) and they all work very well. Even MMM will work if you want it (not tested without Protontricks).

title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes:
A simple guide to getting Fallout 3 to work on Linux using Steam Proton. 

Step 1 – Install Fallout 3 using Steam Proton 4.11-3

Step 2 – Download Xlive.dll

Best place is from NexusMods 

https://www.nexusmods.com/fallout3/mods/22591?tab=files

For standard Fallout 3 copy it to: 

Home / .steam / steam / steamapps / compatdata / 22300 / pfx / drive_c / windows / syswow64

For Fallout 3 – Game of the Year Edition copy it to: 

Home / .steam / steam / steamapps / compatdata / 22370 / pfx / drive_c / windows / syswow64

Step 3 – Fix the problem with mouse and random crashes.

Add / edit the following entries in Fallout.ini – You will need to launch Fallout 3 at least once to generate this file.

For standard Fallout 3 this is found at:

Home / .steam / steam / steamapps / compatdata / 22300 / pfx / drive_c / users / steamuser / My Documents / My Games / Fallout3

For Fallout 3 – Game of the Year Edition this is found at:

Home / .steam / steam / steamapps / compatdata / 22370 / pfx / drive_c / users / steamuser / My Documents / My Games / Fallout3

[General] 
bUseThreadedAI=1
iNumHWThreads=2

[Controls]
fForegroundMouseAccelBase=0
fForegroundMouseAccelTop=0
fForegroundMouseBase=0
fForegroundMouseMult=0

Step 4 – Disable Anti-aliasing and HDR / Bloom

Once done launch the game and have fun playing Fallout 3.

See https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GFCbv7eEF6U for a visual guide.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: The game runs perfectly after some tweaks. 1) follow all the instructions in the report in this page that starts with "A simple guide to getting Fallout 3 to work on Linux using Steam Proton". 2) in some cases is necessary to put "xlive.dll" and "msidcrl40.dll" inside the root game folder in order to run the game (/home/user/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Fallout 3 goty). Do this step only if you did everything first and the game did not run. You can easily find those dlls searching for them on Google. 3) if you use intel graphics as GPU you need to do an extra tweak, Direct3D.reg, (as it was in my case, if you don't do it the game will open the menu, but will crash when start a new game): access this page "https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/370", find the post of a user named "RussianNeuroMancer" and copy the windows registry key that is provided the in a txt file. After that, save and rename the file to any name and change the extension .txt to .reg. 4) open regedit inside fallout 3 proton prefix (you can use protontricks or paste this command in terminal (change "user" to your user name, if you installed the game in other path you will need to adapt the command) "WINEPREFIX=/home/user/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/22370/pfx regedit". 5) in the regedit menu, go to "registry" and "import registry file", select the .reg file you saved. After that the game will run. IMPORTANT: the game crashed when I used D9VK, I did not had to use any proton launch option. The only problem I noticed was that the game did not run in total full screen, in the bottom and right side of my monitor there were some small transparent lines (about 3 or 4 pixels sized) that allow me to see the colours of my wallpaper, is something that does not affect game play.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition

notes: The game runs better than on my old PC with Windows 10 (still newer than the game) and an ATI card.
title: Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition
